name: google-calendar-list-events
description:
List upcoming events from a Google Calendar over a time window. Returns structured event data (summary, start, end, location, attendees, hangout_link). Canonical building block for morning-brief / meeting-prep / schedule-awareness workflows.

Requires a vault:// reference to a Google OAuth access token in the AUTH_HEADER config key. The controller resolves + refreshes the token at dispatch time.

AUTH_HEADER.description: vault:// path to the OAuth access token wrapped in 'Bearer ' (e.g. 'Bearer vault://oauth/google_calendar/{user_id}/{account_id}/access_token'). The controller resolves + refreshes the token if expired.

CALENDAR_ID.description: Calendar ID. 'primary' for the authenticated user's main calendar, or a specific calendar address / ID.

HOURS_AHEAD.description: Window size in hours (1–168, i.e. up to 7 days). Events starting between now and now+HOURS_AHEAD are returned.

setup_instructions.title: Enable the Google Calendar API
setup_instructions.detail: In https://console.cloud.google.com, enable the Google Calendar API and configure OAuth consent with the 'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.readonly' scope.

setup_instructions.title: Authorize the integration
setup_instructions.detail: Complete the OAuth flow via your Talos integration UI or CLI. The controller stores tokens at 'oauth/google_calendar/{user_id}/{account_id}/{access|refresh}_token' and tracks expiry.

setup_instructions.title: Configure AUTH_HEADER
setup_instructions.detail: Set AUTH_HEADER to 'Bearer vault://oauth/google_calendar/{your_user_id}/{your_account_id}/access_token'. For 401s, use refresh_oauth_token(vault_path) to force a refresh and inspect the outcome.
